title: Integration
summary: Free 9709 Pure Maths 3 integration lesson — reverse chain rule, integration by parts, and definite integrals with Cambridge mark-scheme habits.

sections.content: Paper 3 integration is harder than Paper 1: you meet **substitution**, **integration by parts**, and **partial fractions** setups. Marks are often split between choosing the correct method (M1) and executing algebra (A1).
∫ u dv/dx dx = uv − ∫ v du/dx dx. Choose u to simplify when differentiated (LIATE: Log, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trig, Exp).

sections.question: Find ∫ x e^x dx.
sections.solution:
Let u = x, dv/dx = e^x → du/dx = 1, v = e^x.

∫ x e^x dx = x e^x − ∫ e^x dx = x e^x − e^x + C = e^x(x − 1) + C.

sections.content: Evaluate definite integrals by finding the antiderivative, then **substitute limits**. For area between curves, ∫ (top − bottom) dx over the intersection interval. Sketching prevents sign errors.

items: Always include + C for indefinite integrals
Check whether the integrand needs a substitution before parts
ECF can rescue later marks if your u/v choice is stated clearly
Show boundary substitution explicitly for A marks

sections.content: If integration by parts needs a second round, write each line neatly — examiners penalise missing brackets that make v du/dx ambiguous. After integrating, differentiate your answer quickly to sanity-check.

steps.caption: Integration reverses differentiation — find the antiderivative F(x) with F′(x) = f(x).
steps.embedHint: Drag the slider — watch how area accumulates as you change the upper limit.

steps.caption: A definite integral ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx equals the signed area under the curve.
steps.embedHint: Shade the region between x = a and x = b — that area is the integral value.

steps.caption: Evaluate F(b) − F(a) after finding F(x); always substitute limits explicitly.
steps.embedHint: Move both limits and read how the shaded area changes.

steps.caption: Integration by parts: ∫ u dv = uv − ∫ v du — choose u using LIATE.
steps.embedHint: Compare area under a product function with the parts formula result.
